data.content:
Implementation plan for Codex project-level managed skills support:

Summary
- Add `./.agents/skills` as the preferred project-level managed-skill location for Codex.
- Preserve `~/.codex/skills` as the fallback user-level location when `./.agents` is absent.
- Keep doctor/install/fix behavior aligned with existing OpenCode project+user selection semantics.

Repository findings
- `src/managed_skills.rs` currently treats Codex as user-only and already models project+user candidates for OpenCode.
- `doctor_location()` already prefers the first existing candidate and falls back to a user-scoped candidate when no tool root exists, so Codex only needs an added project candidate ordered before the user candidate.
- `preferred_location()` and `fix_doctor_check()` already route through the shared location-selection path, so no command wiring changes are needed once Codex exposes the extra candidate.
- Existing tests in `src/managed_skills_tests.rs` already cover install, doctor, update, and fix behavior for managed skills and can be extended without broad structural changes.

Planned file changes
1. `src/managed_skills.rs`
- In `SkillTool::location_candidates()`, add a Codex project-level candidate at `repo_root.join(".agents")` with `LocationScope::Project` and `skills` as the child directory.
- Order Codex candidates as project first, then user `home.join(".codex")`, so installs and doctor/fix prefer `./.agents/skills` whenever `./.agents` exists.
- Update `expected_root_hint(SkillTool::Codex)` to advertise `.agents or ~/.codex`.

2. `src/managed_skills_tests.rs`
- Add an install test proving Codex writes to `./.agents/skills/...` when `./.agents` exists.
- Add a fallback test proving Codex still selects `~/.codex` when `./.agents` does not exist.
- Add a doctor test proving project-level Codex skills are detected and reported correctly.
- Add a doctor `--fix` test proving drifted or missing Codex skills under `./.agents/skills` are reconciled.
- Update the root-count coverage in `locations_detect_supported_roots_for_all_tools()` so Codex expects both project and user roots when both exist.
- Update missing-root warning expectations to reflect the new Codex hint text.

Validation strategy
- Run the targeted managed-skills test set while implementing to confirm location ordering and doctor/fix behavior.
- Run `make sanity` before completing the implementation step to satisfy lint, test, and coverage gates.

Complexity and risks
- Complexity is low because the required behavior already exists for OpenCode and the shared doctor/fix paths are location-agnostic.
- Main compatibility risk is accidentally changing existing user-level Codex installs; ordering the project candidate first while retaining the user fallback avoids that regression.
- Uninstall behavior should continue to work across both roots because it already iterates all detected locations.

Dependencies and hierarchy
- No additional child knots are required; the work is small and contained to the managed-skills location logic and its tests.
